preamble: The nasalization of the mid-front vowel [e] is a common process in languages where vowels are followed by nasal consonants, particularly in Romance languages.

examples.note: Latin intervocalic [n] was lost in Portuguese, leaving a nasalized vowel (venit > vẽe > vem).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
